rules.name: API Baseurl HTTPS
rules.description: Each API baseURL should be an https URL.

rules.name: API Contact Include Email
rules.description: The contact object in an APIs.json file allows for referencing the email address of a person or group. This property provides a convenient way for users to seek support for an API, using email as the default method to reach the team managing the API in production.

rules.name: API Contact Include Full Name
rules.description: The contact object in an APIs.json file includes a full name (FN) property, which specifies the formatted text corresponding to the contact name in the vCard for an API. This provides a quick and clear way to assign a person, group, or other point of contact to an API for addressing questions and providing support.

rules.name: API Entry Require Tag

rules.name: API Include About Page

rules.name: API Include Contact

rules.name: API Property Require Java SDK

rules.name: API Property Require Linting Rules
rules.description: This property ensures that an API has governance rules applied, usually as part of a central set of governance rules, defined by policy, or stages of the API lifecycle.

rules.name: API Property Require Python SDK
rules.description: This property ensures that there is a Python SDK available for an API, making it easier for Python developers to integrate an API into their applications.

rules.name: API Property Require Sandbox

rules.name: API Property Require SDK
rules.description: This property ensures that there is an SDK available for an API, making it easier for developers to integrate an API into their applications.

rules.name: API Property Require Support Page
rules.description: This property ensures that there is a support page available for an API, providing direct and in-direct support opportunities for each API or for entire API platform.

rules.name: API Property Require Team

rules.name: API Property Require Terms Of Service

rules.name: API Property Require Use Cases

rules.name: API Property Require Valid URL
rules.description: This property ensures that properties of an API or API contract all have valid URLs, checking if any of the URLs are not properly formed, or could be other formats.

rules.name: API Property Require Versioning

rules.name: API Property Require Video

rules.name: API Property Require Vocabulary

rules.name: API Property URL HTTPS

rules.name: API Require Aid

The APIs.json file, contract, or collection contains its own AID, which the API uses as part of its reference. Additionally, the API applies a slugified version of its name, resulting in a unique, human-readable identifier for the API.

rules.name: API Tag Require Upper Case

rules.name: Document APIs JSON URL HTTPS

rules.name: Schema Include API Lifecycle
rules.description: This property makes sure there is an API lifecycle schema defining all of the stages of a lifecycle and which policies get applied at each stage of the API lifecycle.

rules.name: Schema Include API Plan
rules.description: This property provides a link to the dedicated plans page that applies to an API, providing information about access tiers, rate limits, and features available for an API as part of a wider API business plan.

rules.name: Schema Include Authentication Page
rules.description: This property ensures that there is a human readable authentication page available that will provide what type of authentication is used and how it can be applied, as well as any services or tooling that API consumers can use to troubleshoot authentication with APIs.

rules.name: Schema Include Blog
rules.description: This property ensures that an API has a reference to a blog where anyone can find updates and other stories that will help keep API consumers and other stakeholders up to speed on what is happening with an API, and the larger operations.

rules.name: Schema Include Blog Feed
rules.description: This property ensures that blogs in support of APIs have an Atom or RSS feed of posts, allowing for the syndication of updates and information around individual APIs and the operations around them.

rules.name: Schema Include Business Validator
rules.description: This property ensures that there is a link to the validator for the APIs.

rules.name: Schema Include Change Log
rules.description: This property ensures that than an individual API or API operations possesses a change log that catalogs all the changes that have occurred in a recent time frame, with historical and version information available if possible.

rules.name: Schema Include Deprecation Policy
rules.description: This property ensures that an API has a deprecation policy shared as part of the contract, communicating what the lifespan of APIs are, each individual version, as well as communication around the deprecation of APIs.

rules.name: Schema Include Developer Portal
rules.description: This property ensures there a developer portal associated with an API and that you can find a landing page for the API, documentation, SDKs, and other resources.

rules.name: Schema Include Discussion Forum
rules.description: This property ensures that there is a link to a discussion forum, providing a way for consumers and producers to engage and support either other throughout the lifecycle.

rules.name: Schema Include Documentation
rules.description: This property ensures that there is documentation published for an API, and API consumers will have a set of human-readable instructions for onboarding and integrating with HTTP APIs in their applications.

rules.name: Schema Include Examples
rules.description: This property ensures that an API has a reference to a examples for individual APIs or as part of common properties, providing examples and synthentic data that can be used for APIs.

rules.name: Schema Include Feedback Email
rules.description: This property ensures that there is an email available for API consumers to provide feedback.

rules.name: Schema Include Feedback Issues URL
rules.description: This property ensures there is a URL to Git issues specifically for providing feedback.

rules.name: Schema Include Gateway Reference
rules.description: This property ensures that there is a reference to the gateway for an API, referencing where you can manage the configuration for each API.

rules.name: Schema Include Getting Started
rules.description: This property ensures that there is a getting started link available, providing a reference for API consumers to get started with an API is as few steps as possible.

rules.name: Schema Include Github Action
rules.description: This property ensures that a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline is available for an API, providing a link to the pipeline YAML artifact, which can be used to automate and govern the API as part of the build process.

rules.name: Schema Include Github Organization
rules.description: This property ensures that an API is associated with GitHub organization, providing the URL to where you can engage with the operations surrounding an API.

rules.name: Schema Include Github Repository
rules.description: This property ensures that an API possess a reference to a dedicated GitHub repository that is used to manage the Open, but also possible server and client code.

rules.name: Schema Include Governance Policies
rules.description: This property ensures there is a governance policies reference as part of an API contract, usually a common property pointing to a centralized set of policies that get applied.

rules.name: Schema Include Insomnia Collection
rules.description: This property defines an Insomnia collection available for each API, providing executable artifacts that can be used in the Insomnia client for making calls, and executing automation workflows.

rules.name: Schema Include Interface License
rules.description: This property ensures that an API Commons interface license exists for an API, providing a machine-readable reference for an API, as well as data, backend, and front-end code.

rules.name: Schema Include Mock Server
rules.description: This property ensures that an API has a reference to a mock servers for individual APIs or as part of common properties, providing mocked deployments of an API that can be used for making test API calls.

rules.name: Schema Include Openapi Definition
rules.description: This property ensures that there is an OpenAPI present for an API, providing the technical contract that describes the surface area of an API.

rules.name: Schema Include Openapi Governance Rules
rules.description: This property ensures that an OpenAPI has support governance rules, that can be applied during design time via editors, development time via IDE, and run-time via CI/CD pipelines.

rules.name: Schema Include Openapi Validator
rules.description: This property ensures that there is a link to the validator for the OpenAPI technical contract, allowing anyone to see the details of governance being applied.

rules.name: Schema Include Operational Rules
rules.description: This property ensures that an API has operational level rules for APIs.

rules.name: Schema Include Performance Testing
rules.description: This property ensures that an API has performance testing in place, providing a URL to the performance testing, dashboard, or other resource.

rules.name: Schema Include Postman Collection
rules.description: This property ensures that an API has at least one Postman Collection associated with it, providing automation, tests, and other executable derivatives of an APIs OpenAPI.

rules.name: Schema Include Postman Public Workspace
rules.description: This property ensures that an API is associated with a Postman Workspace, providing a single location that API producers and/or API consumers can engage around an API.

rules.name: Schema Include Pricing
rules.description: This property provides a link to a pricing page that applies to an API, providing a breakdown of the costs associated with using an API.

rules.name: Schema Include Privacy Policy
rules.description: This property provides a link to the privacy policy for an API, providing the legal details of how privacy is approached for each API.

rules.name: Schema Include Production Environment
rules.description: This property ensures that there is a production environment available for an API, providing base URL, tokens, keys, and other key / value pairs that are needed to integrate with an API.

rules.name: Schema Include Questions Issues URL
rules.description: This property ensures that an API has a dedicated link to Git issues for asking questions.

rules.name: Schema Include Rate Limit Reference
rules.description: This property ensures there is an API rate limits reference associated with API, ensuring the rate limits applied to an API are clearly communicated.

rules.name: Schema Include Staging Environment
rules.description: This property ensures that there is a staging environment available for an API, providing base URL, tokens, keys, and other key / value pairs that are needed to integrate with an API.

rules.name: Schema Include Version Comparison
rules.description: This property ensures that an API has the ability to compare two different versions of an API and see what the difference are between them.

rules.name: Tag Require Upper Case

arazzo.label: Arazzo
arazzo.description: API workflow descriptions — multi-step calls across operations.

rules.name: Arazzo Document Schema
rules.description: Arazzo Document must be valid against the Arazzo schema.

rules.name: Arazzo Info Description
rules.description: Info "description" should be present and non-empty string.

rules.name: Arazzo Info Summary
rules.description: Info "summary" is recommended be present and be a non-empty string.

rules.name: Arazzo No Script Tags In Markdown
rules.description: Markdown descriptions must not have "<script>" tags.

rules.name: Arazzo Source Descriptions Type
rules.description: Source Description "type" should be present.

rules.name: Arazzo Step Description
rules.description: Step "description" should be present and non-empty string.

rules.name: Arazzo Step Failure Actions Validation
rules.description: Every failure action must have a unique "name", and the fields "workflowId" and "stepId" are mutually exclusive.

rules.name: Arazzo Step OperationPath
rules.description: It is recommended to use "operationId" rather than "operationPath".

rules.name: Arazzo Step Output Validation
rules.description: Every step output must have unique name and its value must be a valid runtime expression.

rules.name: Arazzo Step Parameters Validation
rules.description: Step parameters and workflow parameters must valid.

rules.name: Arazzo Step Request Body Validation
rules.description: Every step request body must have a valid `contentType` and use of runtime expressions.

rules.name: Arazzo Step StepId
rules.description: Step "stepId" should follow the pattern "^[A-Za-z0-9_\-]+$".

rules.name: Arazzo Step Success Actions Validation
rules.description: Every success action must have a unique "name", and the fields "workflowId" and "stepId" are mutually exclusive.

rules.name: Arazzo Step Success Criteria Validation
rules.description: Every success criteria must have a valid context, conditions, and types.

rules.name: Arazzo Step Validation
rules.description: Every step must have a valid "stepId" and an valid "operationId" or "operationPath" or "workflowId".

rules.name: Arazzo Workflow Depends On Validation
rules.description: Every workflow dependency must be valid.

rules.name: Arazzo Workflow Description
rules.description: Workflow "description" should be present and non-empty string.

rules.name: Arazzo Workflow Output Validation
rules.description: Every workflow output must have unique name and its value must be a valid runtime expression.

rules.name: Arazzo Workflow StepId Unique
rules.description: Every step must have unique "stepId".

rules.name: Arazzo Workflow Summary
rules.description: Workflow "summary" should be present and non-empty string.

rules.name: Arazzo Workflow WorkflowId
rules.description: Workflow "workflowId" should follow the pattern "^[A-Za-z0-9_\-]+$".

rules.name: Arazzo WorkflowId Unique
rules.description: Every workflow must have unique "workflowId".

rules.name: Document Require Arazzo
rules.description: arazzo document should declare a `arazzo` property.

rules.name: Document Require Component
rules.description: arazzo document should declare a `components` property.

rules.name: Document Require Info
rules.description: arazzo document should declare a `info` property.

rules.name: Document Require Sourcedescriptions
rules.description: arazzo document should declare a `sourceDescriptions` property.

rules.name: Document Require Workflow
rules.description: arazzo document should declare a `workflows` property.

rules.name: Info Arazzo Require Title
rules.description: The Arazzo description should declare an info.title.

rules.name: Source Arazzo Description Require Name
rules.description: Each source description should declare a name used to reference it from steps.

rules.name: Source Arazzo Description Require URL
rules.description: Each source description should declare a url to the referenced OpenAPI/Arazzo document.

rules.name: Workflow Arazzo Require Step
rules.description: A workflow should define at least one step.

rules.name: Workflow Arazzo Step Require Operation
rules.description: Each step should reference an operation via operationId, operationPath, or a nested workflowId.

rules.name: Workflow Arazzo Step Require Success Criteria
rules.description: Steps should declare successCriteria so the workflow can assert each step succeeded.

agent-skill.label: Agent Skill
agent-skill.description: Agent skills — SKILL.md metadata and tooling.

rules.name: Skill Allowed Tools Array
rules.description: If a skill declares allowed-tools, it must be an array of tool names so its capability surface is explicit and auditable.

rules.name: Skill Body Length
rules.description: Skill bodies should stay focused — very long instructions degrade agent performance. Keep the body under ~5000 words.

rules.name: Skill Body Present
rules.description: A skill must have body content, not just frontmatter — the body is the instruction set the agent actually follows.

rules.name: Skill Description
rules.description: A skill must declare a description — it is the primary signal an agent uses to decide when to invoke the skill.

rules.name: Skill Description Length
rules.description: Skill descriptions should be specific enough to drive good selection — at least 40 characters of meaningful intent.

rules.name: Skill Description Single Line
rules.description: A skill description should be a single line (no embedded line breaks) so it renders cleanly in pickers.

rules.name: Skill Has Examples Section
rules.description: A skill body should show concrete examples under an Examples section so agents and humans can see how it is meant to be invoked.

rules.name: Skill Has Usage Section
rules.description: A skill body should document how to use it under a Usage section, so both humans and agents can see how it is meant to be invoked.

rules.name: Skill License
rules.description: A skill should declare a license so consumers know the terms under which they can reuse and redistribute it.

rules.name: Skill Metadata Object
rules.description: If a skill declares metadata, it should be an object of key/value pairs.

rules.name: Skill Name
rules.description: A skill must declare a name in its frontmatter — it identifies the skill to agents and tooling.

rules.name: Skill Name Kebab Case
rules.description: Skill name should be lower kebab-case so it matches the skill directory name and stays portable.

rules.name: Skill Name Max Length
rules.description: A skill name should be concise (64 characters or fewer).

rules.name: Skill Require Allowed Tools
rules.description: agent-skill document should declare a `allowed-tools` property.

rules.name: Skill Require Metadata
rules.description: agent-skill document should declare a `metadata` property.

rules.name: Skill Require Version
rules.description: agent-skill document should declare a `version` property.

rules.name: Skill Single Title
rules.description: A skill body should have at most one level-1 heading — the frontmatter name is the title, and sections should use level-2 headings.

rules.name: Skill Version Semver
rules.description: If a skill declares a version, it should be semantic (e.g. 1.0.0) so consumers can reason about changes between releases.
